payload.title: Gate readiness on DB availability with minimal reversible edits
payload.approach: Update readiness logic to depend on database availability, add focused unit coverage, and validate with existing npm scripts.
payload.rationale: This change is reversible, constrained to health gating behavior, and avoids touching unrelated runtime, deployment, or dependency surfaces.

acceptanceCoverage.strategy: Modify `HealthService.readiness()` to return true only when process readiness is true and `DatabaseHealth.isAvailable()` resolves true.
Handle `isAvailable()` rejection or error by returning readiness false and preserving a deterministic boolean result.
Keep liveness and startup method signatures and behavior unchanged, limiting implementation edits to readiness semantics and test setup.
Add tests in `test/health-service.test.ts` that assert readiness false under DB unavailability and true when DB is available while process state is running.
Verify success through existing scripts `npm run build` and `npm test`.

invariantProtection.strategy: Do not change public API types or signatures in `HealthService` or `DatabaseHealth`.
Do not add production dependencies and keep strict TypeScript behavior unchanged.
Limit behavioral change to readiness gating only so startup/liveness and non-health deployment settings remain unchanged.
Avoid editing `deployment.json` probe definitions in this plan unless explicitly approved.

files.purpose: Implement DB-gated readiness behavior in HealthService.
Add coverage for readiness behavior under DB availability and unavailability.

steps.description: Inspect current readiness and probe implementations plus `DatabaseHealth` contract to confirm minimal insertion points for DB availability checks.
Implement readiness as a process-ready plus DB-availability check with strict failure-to-false handling.
Add/adjust unit tests for DB true/false readiness outcomes while preserving existing liveness/startup assertions.
Run build and test scripts to confirm no regressions before merge.

safetyTests.name: Full test script execution
safetyTests.proves: Regression coverage for readiness behavior passes through the configured npm test entrypoint.

verificationCommands.name: Build
verificationCommands.purpose: Validate strict TypeScript build after service and test changes.

verificationCommands.name: Test
verificationCommands.purpose: Validate complete repository test suite through the configured npm script.

risks: If DB availability checks are slow or intermittent, readiness may flap and temporarily reduce routing capacity.
Transient DB failures can reduce readiness even when other subsystems are healthy, so probe tuning may be needed after rollout.
assumptions: Readiness at `/health/ready` is the effective traffic-routing gate for this deployment.
Database availability checks are safe to perform inside readiness without unacceptable probe overhead.
`npm test` and `npm run build` remain stable validation entrypoints for this repo.

unknowns.description: Whether upstream infrastructure uses additional routing inputs beyond Kubernetes readiness (for example gateways or service mesh logic).
unknowns.resolution: Confirm with platform/runtime owners before rollout and document evidence if divergence exists.

unknowns.description: Whether external routing components already bypass readiness probes in this environment.
unknowns.resolution: Verify deployment runtime behavior in environment-specific docs or tests during rollout.

recovery: Revert the changed readiness branch in `src/health-service.ts` to process-only readiness and remove newly added DB-specific readiness assertions from `test/health-service.test.ts`.
If validation fails, isolate and revert just the new readiness code path and rerun `npm run build` plus `npm test`.
